messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-planning agent. Prepare a compact context pack for formalizing a LaTeX theorem-like source unit. The target Lean declarations aligned to the selected source unit are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "rules": [
    "Do not infer or reveal hidden target Lean declaration names for the selected unit.",
    "Do not write theorem/lemma Lean code in target_statement_sketch; exact API syntax belongs in needed_mathlib_context and will be hydrated by tools.",
    "Do not bundle all source parts into one conjunction unless the source unit itself requires that shape.",
    "Use previous project declarations only if they are shown under prior_project_context.",
    "Do not treat Mathlib as the only context; enumerate source/project/local/Mathlib context separately.",
    "Prefer exact Mathlib names when known; otherwise give a narrow query plus expected signature shape.",
    "Keep added context tight: prefer a few thousand tokens or less per source unit."
  ],
  "schema": {
    "units": [

        "context_pack_size_risk": "low|medium|high",

  "task": "For each LaTeX source unit, decompose the source into ordered Lean declaration tasks and select tight context for each task. The context inventory must be separate: source text, previous book/source statements, previous project declarations, local file/import/style context, and selected Mathlib APIs.",

        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "lemma",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Details/InfiniteProducts1.tex:lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div",
            "labels": [
              "lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              388,
              407
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Details/InfiniteProducts1.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div}Let $a,b,c,d\\in K\\left[  \\left[  x\\right]\n\\right]  $ be four FPSs such that $c$ and $d$ are invertible. Let\n$n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Assume that\n\\[\n\\left[  x^{m}\\right]  a=\\left[  x^{m}\\right]  b\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{for\neach }m\\in\\left\\{  0,1,\\ldots,n\\right\\}  .\n\\]\nAssume further that%\n\\[\n\\left[  x^{m}\\right]  c=\\left[  x^{m}\\right]  d\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{for\neach }m\\in\\left\\{  0,1,\\ldots,n\\right\\}  .\n\\]\nThen,\n\\[\n\\left[  x^{m}\\right]  \\dfrac{a}{c}=\\left[  x^{m}\\right]  \\dfrac{b}%\n{d}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{for each }m\\in\\left\\{  0,1,\\ldots,n\\right\\}  .\n\\]\n\n\\end{lemma}"
          }
        }
      ],
      "prior_project_context": [
        {
          "project_declarations": [
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib",
                "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.InfiniteProducts",
                "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.XnEquivalence"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- If coefficients agree up to n for numerators and invertible denominators,\n    quotients agree up to n.\n    (Lemma lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div)\n    Label: lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div -/\ntheorem xnEquiv_div {n : ℕ} {a b c d : K⟦X⟧}\n    (hab : a ≡[x^n] b) (hcd : c ≡[x^n] d)\n    (hc : IsUnit (constantCoeff c)) (hd : IsUnit (constantCoeff d)) :\n    a * invOfUnit c hc.unit ≡[x^n] b * invOfUnit d hd.unit",
              "line_range": [
                149,
                172
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.InfiniteProducts1",
              "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.xnEquiv_div",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/InfiniteProducts1.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib",
                "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.XnEquivalence"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- If two pairs of FPSs agree on their first n+1 coefficients, and the denominators are\ninvertible, then their quotients also agree on those coefficients.\n(Lemma 7.3.16, label: lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div)\n\nThis is a direct consequence of the fact that the k-th coefficient of a quotient\nonly depends on coefficients 0 through k of each factor.\n\nThis is an alternative formulation of `xnEquiv_div` using explicit coefficient equality. -/\ntheorem coeff_div_eq_of_coeff_eq {a b c d : PowerSeries K} {n : ℕ}\n    (hab : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m a = coeff m b)\n    (hcd : ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m c = coeff m d)\n    (u : Kˣ) (hu : constantCoeff c = u)\n    (v : Kˣ) (hv : constantCoeff d = v) :\n    ∀ m ≤ n, coeff m (a * invOfUnit c u) = coeff m (b * invOfUnit d v)",
              "line_range": [
                457,
                471
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.Limits",
              "name": "PowerSeries.coeff_div_eq_of_coeff_eq",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/Limits.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [
                "lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div"
              ],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            }
          ],
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
          "source_labels": [
            "lem.fps.prod.irlv.cong-div"
          ],

        }

      },
      "source_unit": {
        "environment": "lemma",
        "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Details/InfiniteProducts1.tex:lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr",
        "labels": [

        "parse_warnings": [],
        "part_markers": [],
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Details/InfiniteProducts1.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr}Let $\\left(  \\mathbf{a}_{i}\\right)\n_{i\\in I}\\in K\\left[  \\left[  x\\right]  \\right]  ^{I}$ be a family of\ninvertible FPSs. Let $J$ be a subset of $I$. Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $U$ be\nan $x^{n}$-approximator for $\\left(  \\mathbf{a}_{i}\\right)  _{i\\in I}$. Then,\n$U\\cap J$ is an $x^{n}$-approximator for $\\left(  \\mathbf{a}_{i}\\right)\n_{i\\in J}$.\n\\end{lemma}"
      },
      "unit_key": "unit-001"
    },

              "lean_snippet": "/-- The intersection of an approximator with a subset gives an approximator for the subfamily.\n    (Lemma lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr)\n    Label: lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr\n\n    Given `U` an x^n-approximator for `(aᵢ)_{i∈I}` and `J ⊆ I` (as a Finset),\n    there exists an x^n-approximator for `(aᵢ)_{i∈J}` derived from `U ∩ J`. -/\ntheorem xnApproximator_inter {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] {a : ι → K⟦X⟧} {n : ℕ}\n    {U : Finset ι} {J : Finset ι}\n    (hU : IsXnApproximator a n U)\n    (hinv : ∀ i, IsUnit (constantCoeff (a i))) :\n    ∃ M : Finset J, IsXnApproximator (fun i : J => a i) n M",
              "line_range": [
                294,
                425
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.InfiniteProducts1",
              "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.xnApproximator_inter",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/InfiniteProducts1.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [],

            {
              "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",

              "kind": "lemma",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Key lemma: If U is an x^n-approximator for the full family, and all FPS are invertible,\nthen U ∩ J is an x^n-approximator for the subfamily J.\nThis is Lemma lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr from the tex source.\nThe invertibility assumption is essential for this lemma. -/\nlemma isXnApproximator_inter_subfamily {a : I → PowerSeries R}\n    {U : Finset I} {n : ℕ}\n    (hU : IsXnApproximator a U n)\n    (ha_inv : ∀ i, IsUnit ((a i).coeff 0))\n    (J : Set I) :\n    IsXnApproximator (fun i : J => a i) \n      (U.preimage (Subtype.val) (Subtype.val_injective.injOn)) n",
              "line_range": [
                1615,

              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.InfiniteProducts",
              "name": "PowerSeries.isXnApproximator_inter_subfamily",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/InfiniteProducts.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [
                "lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr"

            {
              "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "prop.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1"

              "imports": [

              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- The family of subproducts indexed by fibers is multipliable.\n(Label: prop.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1)\n\nThis theorem proves that if a family `(a_s)_{s ∈ S}` of power series is multipliable,\nand each fiber `{s : f s = w}` gives a multipliable subfamily, then the family of\nfiber products `(tprod_{s : f s = w} a_s)_{w ∈ W}` is also multipliable.\n\n**Note:** The TeX source proof uses Lemma lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr which\nrequires invertibility. This Lean proof uses a different approach that avoids the\ninvertibility assumption: instead of showing each fiber's tprod is x^n-equivalent to 1\n(which requires invertibility to \"cancel\" other fibers), we directly relate the outer\nproduct to the full product via unions of fiber approximators. -/\ntheorem multipliable_prod_fibers {S W : Type*} {f : S → W} {a : S → PowerSeries R}\n    (ha : Multipliable a)\n    (ha_fibers : ∀ w : W, Multipliable (fun s : {s : S // f s = w} => a s)) :\n    Multipliable (fun w : W => tprod (fun s : {s : S // f s = w} => a s) (ha_fibers w))",
              "line_range": [
                1934,
                2047
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.FPS.InfiniteProducts",
              "name": "PowerSeries.multipliable_prod_fibers",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/FPS/InfiniteProducts.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [
                "lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr"
              ],

              "snippet_truncated": true

              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- The family of subproducts indexed by fibers is multipliable (invertible case).\n(Label: prop.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1)\n\nThis is a version of `multipliable_prod_fibers` with an invertibility assumption that\nenables a complete proof. The tex source proof implicitly uses invertibility through\nLemma `lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr`.\n\nWhen all FPS in the family have unit constant term, we can use `coeff_eq_of_mul_eq_unit`\nto cancel common factors and relate fiber products. -/\ntheorem multipliable_prod_fibers_inv {S W : Type*} {f : S → W} {a : S → PowerSeries R}\n    (ha : Multipliable a)\n    (ha_inv : ∀ s, IsUnit ((a s).coeff 0))\n    (ha_fibers : ∀ w : W, Multipliable (fun s : {s : S // f s = w} => a s)",

              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- A multipliable product can be broken into subproducts indexed by a map (invertible case).\n(Label: prop.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1)\n\nThis is a version of `tprod_eq_tprod_fibers` with an invertibility assumption that\nenables a complete proof. The tex source proof implicitly uses invertibility through\nLemma `lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr`.\n\nWhen all FPS in the family have unit constant term, we can use `coeff_eq_of_mul_eq_unit`\nto cancel common factors and relate fiber products. -/\ntheorem tprod_eq_tprod_fibers_inv {S W : Type*} {f : S → W} {a : S → PowerSeries R}\n    (ha : Multipliable a)\n    (ha_inv : ∀ s, IsUnit ((a s).coeff 0))\n    (ha_fibers : ∀ w : W, Multipliable (fun s : {s : S // f s = w} => a s)",

              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Subfamilies of a multipliable family of invertible FPSs are multipliable.\n    (Proposition prop.fps.union-mulable (a) / prop.fps.prods-mulable-subfams)\n    Label: prop.fps.union-mulable\n\n    Note: This is also known as `multipliable_subfamily` in the source.\n    The proof uses `xnApproximator_inter` (lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr). -/\ntheorem multipliable_of_subset {ι : Type*} {a : ι → K⟦X⟧} {J : Set ι}\n    (h : Multipliable a) (hinv : ∀ i, IsUnit (constantCoeff (a i))) :\n    Multipliable (fun i : J => a i)",

              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- For a surjection `f : S → W`, if the original family and all fiber families\n    are multipliable, then the family of fiber products is multipliable.\n    (Proposition prop.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1, Claim 1 setup)\n    Label: prop.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1\n\n    Note: The proof requires invertibility of each `a s` (i.e., `IsUnit (constantCoeff (a s))`).\n    This is used in Claim 2 of the tex proof, which applies `lem.fps.prods-mulable-subfams-appr`\n    to show that `U ∩ fiber_w` is an x^n-approximator for the fiber subfamily. -/\ntheorem multipliable_fiber_prods {S W : Type*} {a : S → K⟦X⟧} {f : S → W}\n    (hS : Multipliable a)\n    (hinv : ∀ s, IsUnit (constantCoeff (a s)))\n    (hfiber : ∀ w : W, Multipliable (fun s : {s : S // f s = w} => a s.1)) :\n    Multipliable (fun w => infprod (fun s : {s : S // f s = w} => a s.1) (hfiber w))",

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Details/InfiniteProducts1.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.fps.prods-mulable-rules.SW1.lem1}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $V$ be a\nfinite set. Let $\\left(  c_{w}\\right)  _{w\\in V}\\in K\\left[  \\left[  x\\right]\n\\right]  ^{V}$ and $\\left(  d_{w}\\right)  _{w\\in V}\\in K\\left[  \\left[\nx\\right]  \\right]  ^{V}$ be two families of FPSs such that\n\\[\n\\text{each }w\\in V\\text{ satisfies }c_{w}\\overset{x^{n}}{\\equiv}d_{w}.\n\\]\nThen, we have%\n\\[\n\\prod_{w\\in V}c_{w}\\overset{x^{n}}{\\equiv}\\prod_{w\\in V}d_{w}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{lemma}"
      },

    }
